subject: Project Management
title: The Second-System Effect
description: A 60-second lesson on The Second-System Effect.

hook.text: Fred Brooks warned: the second version of any system is almost always bloated and overengineered.

buildup.text: After a successful v1, designers pack v2 with every feature they wished they'd included the first time.

discovery.text: Windows Vista, Netscape 6, and the Airbus A380 all suffered from second-system syndrome.

twist.text: The irony: success breeds overconfidence. The team that nailed v1 often destroys v2 with ambition.

climax.text: The second system teaches that restraint matters more the second time — not less.

quiz.question: What is the second-system effect?
options: Version 2 of a system is often bloated with too many features
Systems need two teams to succeed
